mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly self-hosted AI platform designed for seamless integration with various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It offers a powerful offline deployment solution with extensibility and built-in inference capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for self-hosting and management of resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage your deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with your new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and configures your kubeconfig.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> Installs Open WebUI in the specified namespace.
commands: helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment open-webui --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace open-webui

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/monitoring/monitoring.yaml --namespace open-webui

```

> Sets up horizontal pod autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Applies monitoring configuration for Open WebUI.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI and associated resources. This will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> Uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui

```

> Backs up the current deployment configuration.
> Upgrades Open WebUI to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Review the deployment configuration and logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or the port is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and ensure the correct port is exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models and improved API integration capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui
gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
